headline: BONANZA: West Red Lake Gold Drills 215.46 g/t Au Over 5.35m at Madsen — Austin 904 Complex Emerging as New Zone
snippet: April 13 assays from the Austin 904 Complex at Red Lake include 388.90 g/t gold over 2.95m. Management sees 904 as a primary drilling focus for all of 2026, with initial production from this zone targeted for H1 2027.

headline: CHINA MOVES: Zijin Mining Pays C$5.5B For Allied Gold — 400,000 oz/yr African Output in 2026's Biggest Gold M&A
snippet: All-cash offer at C$44.00/share — a 27% premium — cleared the Allied Gold shareholder vote, with closing expected late April 2026. Adds producing mines in Mali, Ivory Coast, and Ethiopia to Zijin's global portfolio.

headline: LITHIUM AWAKENS: Core Lithium Locks In $205M to Restart Finniss — Li Carbonate Prices Double From 2025 Lows
snippet: Funding includes a Glencore convertible note, Nebari debt facility, and A$120M equity raise. China's April 2026 VAT rebate cut — from 9% to 6%, with full elimination in 2027 — has pushed lithium carbonate to $16,700–$22,500/t.

headline: Americas Gold & Silver Hits 4,896 g/t Ag at Galena — Launches Biggest-Ever 64,000m Drill Campaign
snippet: Ten new high-grade silver-copper-antimony veins discovered in Idaho including 4,896 g/t silver and 3.95% copper over 1.3m. The 64,000m campaign across Galena and Cosalá is the largest drill program in company history.

headline: Gold Slips to $4,723 as US Eyes Hormuz Blockade — Still Up 47% Year-Over-Year After Peaking at $4,850
snippet: Spot gold fell 0.59% on Monday as US plans to blockade the Strait of Hormuz following failed Iran talks rattled markets. Gold hit $4,850 last Tuesday on ceasefire hopes before retreating; it remains up 47% year-over-year.

headline: Silver CLIMBS to $75.60 — Third Straight Weekly Gain as China's 790+ Tonne Binge Signals Tightening Supply
snippet: China imported over 790 tonnes of silver in early 2026, largely driven by solar manufacturing. A weaker dollar and US-Iran diplomatic progress are driving silver's three-week winning streak, which saw a peak of $77/oz last week.

headline: NexGen Starts Rook I Construction; Denison Gets Wheeler River Nod — First New Canadian Uranium Mines Since 2004
snippet: CNSC approved NexGen's Rook I construction licence March 5 and Denison's Wheeler River February 19. Combined projects represent $2.8B investment and 1,600+ construction jobs in northern Saskatchewan.

headline: Trump Declares Uranium a NATIONAL SECURITY Risk — DOE Commits $2.7B to Domestic Enrichment Capacity
snippet: Section 232 proclamation empowers tariffs, price floors, and potential equity stakes in domestic uranium miners. The DOE commitment targets reduced reliance on Russian and Central Asian enrichment over the next decade.

headline: Copper Holds Near $5.87/lb But LME Stockpiles Hit 8-YEAR HIGH — Demand Warning Flashes Behind the Rally
snippet: LME copper inventories climbed to their highest level since 2018, raising questions about near-term demand even as prices post a third consecutive weekly advance tied to Iran ceasefire optimism and tariff hedging.

headline: The Gilded Rotation: GDX Up 180%, GDXJ Up 203% in 12 Months — Gold Miners Enter RARE Outperformance Mode
snippet: After years of lagging bullion, gold mining equities broke out to all-time highs in late February 2026. Analysts say generalist funds remain underweight, leaving room for continued rotation into mining stocks as margins hit record levels.

headline: Mining M&A Hits 13-YEAR HIGH — $93.7B in 2025 Deals, Mega-Deals Up 68%; Gold Commands 77% of Activity
snippet: Global mining M&A reached its highest total since 2012, with gold and silver assets dominating 77% of all activity. Zijin/Allied Gold, Coeur/New Gold, and the Anglo American/Teck deal led the consolidation wave.

headline: AbraSilver Kicks Off Phase VI at Diablillos — DFS Due Q2 2026 After Phase V Hit 31m @ 9.96 g/t Au
snippet: Phase VI at Argentina's Diablillos project targets resource expansion at Oculto East and Cerro Bayo, building on Phase V's top hole of 31m at 9.96 g/t gold. A definitive feasibility study is scheduled for Q2 2026 release.

headline: Gold Fields Says It's 'Open to Further M&A Growth' — Senior Miners Join 2026 Acquisition Feeding Frenzy
snippet: Gold Fields management signaled openness to additional acquisitions as major producers pursue reserve replacement amid gold's historic rally. The move follows sector M&A hitting a 13-year high in 2025.
